subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the growth and challenges of the cherry tree planted by Rakesh
Evaluate how patience, care, and time contribute to growth in nature
Interpret the symbolic meaning of the cherry tree in the story
Examine the relationship between humans and the natural environment
Develop higher-order reading comprehension and analytical thinking
Reflect on how perseverance and resilience help overcome difficulties

lesson_plan.title: Nature, Growth, and Human Responsibility
topics_covered: Importance of planting and protecting trees
Seeds as symbols of growth and potential
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation with real-life environmental examples
student_activities: Discuss how planting a tree affects the environment
Analyze why a small seed can represent future growth

lesson_plan.title: Setting, Characters, and Context
student_activities: Describe the setting of the story using textual evidence
Analyze the relationship between Rakesh and his grandfather

lesson_plan.title: Development of the Cherry Tree
topics_covered: Care and attention given by Rakesh
student_activities: Create a timeline of the cherry tree’s growth
Explain how Rakesh’s actions influence the tree’s survival

lesson_plan.title: Conflict and Resilience in Nature
topics_covered: Tree being cut by the grass cutter
The resilience of the cherry tree
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of resilience in nature
student_activities: Analyze how the tree survives different challenges
Compare the tree’s struggle with human perseverance

lesson_plan.title: Climax, Symbolism, and Theme
topics_covered: Theme of patience, growth, and harmony with nature
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of symbolism and theme
student_activities: Interpret the symbolic meaning of the cherry tree
Write a short analytical paragraph on the theme of the story

lesson_plan.title: Language Analysis and Reflection
topics_covered: Narrative techniques used in the story
student_activities: Identify examples of imagery in the story
Analyze how descriptive language creates a vivid picture of nature

teaching_aids: Images of cherry trees and Himalayan landscapes
teacher_tip: Encourage students to view the cherry tree as a symbol of patience, resilience, and the deep connection between humans and nature. Discuss how small actions, such as planting a seed, can lead to meaningful long-term outcomes.
